Google just dropped a massive update to Gemini CLI and honestly it changes everything. Your terminal is no longer just a terminal. It's becoming a full AI development team. We're talking plan mode, research agents, Shopify integration, Canva integration. AI agents that talk to each other and build entire projects for you. No more AI randomly rewriting your code without asking. No more chaos. No more surprises. just clean, controlled AI powered development. And the best part, I'm going to show you exactly how to use this for your business today. Real examples, real workflows, no fluff. This is the update developers and entrepreneurs have been waiting for. Let's get into it. Hey, if we haven't met already, I'm the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of SEO agency Goldie Agency. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian Goldie reads every comment, so make sure you comment below and let him know what you want to see next. Today, we're breaking down the Gemini CLI v0.03.0 update that just dropped on March 11th, 2026. This is one of the biggest updates Google has released for developers and AI builders. And by the end of this video, you're going to understand exactly why it matters and exactly how to use it. Stay till the end because the agent architecture section alone will blow your mind. Let's start with the problem because before this update, AI coding agents had one massive issue that drove developers absolutely crazy. You'd ask the AI to fix a small bug, something simple, one line maybe, and what would happen? The AI would go rogue. It would start rewriting files you never asked it to touch, restructuring things that were working perfectly fine, changing logic you spent hours building, and by the time it was done, your project looked nothing like it did before. You'd sit there wondering what just happened to that was the core problem. AI coding agents were powerful but unpredictable. No planning, no transparency, no safety net, just raw execution with zero guardrails. And for anyone building something serious, that is terrifying. You can't run a business on tools you can't trust. So Google went back and fixed it. And the fix is called plan mode. The biggest feature plan mode is the headline feature of this update. And for good reason. It completely changes how the AI interacts with your codebase. Here's how it works. Instead of jumping straight into editing your files, the AI first stops and thinks. It scans your entire project. It understands the architecture. It builds a full step-by-step plan of everything it wants to do. Then it shows you that plan, every single task in order, and it waits. It does not touch a single file until you say go. So, the workflow looks like this. AI analyzes the codebase. AI builds the plan, AI presents the tasks, you review and approve, then AI executes. That is a completely different experience. That is an AI you can actually trust. And it gets better because plan mode now includes built-in research sub aents. So before it even writes the plan, it's doing deep research on your project, understanding the context and building something accurate and relevant rather than guessing. To activate it, you just hit shift plus tab on your keyboard. that toggles you between normal mode except changes mode and plan mode or you can type the /command hog plan directly in the terminal simple as that and in the next release via point 34 plan mode is going to be switched on by default so this is where AI development is heading as the new standard now let me show you what this looks like in a real business context because theory is great but practical examples are what actually help you learn let's say you're building out the AI profit boardroom membership platform form. You want to create a new onboarding sequence for members who just joined. Something that gets them to their first win quickly, keeps them engaged, and makes sure they actually stick around long term. This is a real problem every community owner faces. Retention starts at on boarding. So, you open Gemini CLI, activate plan mode, and you type something like this. [snorts] Analyze the current onboarding flow for the AI profit boardroom. Identify the drop off points where new members lose momentum. Then build a plan to create an improved onboarding sequence that gets members to their first valuable action within 48 hours. And what happens? The AI scans the whole project structure. It maps everything out. It identifies the gaps and it comes back to you with a full plan, tasks in order, nothing executed yet. You read through it. You adjust anything you want to change. You hit approve and then it builds. That is how you use AI without losing control of your project. Quick pause here. If you want to learn how to apply AI tools like Gemini CLI inside a real business workflow, the AI profit boardroom is exactly where you need to be. It's a community for entrepreneurs and business owners who are serious about using AI automation to grow. Inside you'll find workflows, tools, strategies, and a group of people who are actually implementing this stuff every day. Not just talking about it, doing it. Go check out the link in the description and come join us. Now, let's talk slash commands because these are seriously underrated. This update brought in a set of powerful new commands that turn Gemini CLI into a proper command center for your entire development workflow. You've got Yout Memory, which lets the AI retain context across sessions, so you never have to reexplain your project from scratch. You've got our extensions, which lets you manage all your add-ons directly from the terminal. You've got a in it for fast project setup and might restore for recovering a previous session if something crashes or goes wrong midbu. The memory command especially is a gamecher for anyone working on ongoing projects. Imagine coming back to work on the AI profit boardroom platform after a week away. Normally you'd have to rebrief the AI on the whole project. Goals, structure, context, everything. With on memory enabled, the AI already knows. It picks up right where you left off. That alone saves serious time across a whole week of work. This is the part I promised would blow your mind. The A2A system, agentto agent architecture. This update brings significant upgrades to how AI agents communicate with each other inside the CLI. You can now run multiple authenticated remote agents simultaneously and they talk to each other, share information, and coordinate tasks in real time. What does that actually mean in practice? Picture this workflow for a development project. You've got one AI agent doing all the research and planning, a second agent writing the code, a third agent running tests and catching bugs, a fourth agent handling deployment. All of them running in parallel, all connected, all reporting back through the CLI. You sit at the top reviewing the plan, approving the steps, steering the direction. The AI does the execution. That is not science fiction. That is Gemini CLI right now. And it is where the entire industry is heading. The developers and entrepreneurs who understand how to direct and orchestrate AI agent systems like this are going to have an enormous advantage over the next few years. Here's what I want you to take away from this video. This update is not just about code. It is about how AI is evolving from a tool that answers questions into a system that plans, executes, and manages complex workflows. The terminal is becoming an operating system for AI agents and Gemini CLI is one of the most powerful examples of that shift happening right now. Whether you are a developer, an agency owner, a community builder, or someone just learning how to use AI in your business, the pattern is the same. Learn to direct AI systems clearly. Review the plan before you approve it. Use the tools available to automate the repetitive work. That is the skill set that matters right now. All right, two things before you go.
